goals.description: Send a final, unambiguous text to teammate_A confirming the file is 'returned' and demanding they personally upload it by 19:00, explicitly stating that any further delay results in me dropping their contribution entirely and keeping only my own work.
goals.rationale: The previous message was too vague ('M…'). I need to cut through the ambiguity immediately. My grade hinges on this file being there by 19:00, and I cannot afford another round of negotiation or excuses. Assertiveness means setting a hard boundary now: either they do it, or I walk away from their part. If they don't comply instantly, I will execute my plan to complete my share perfectly anyway, effectively forcing them to lose credit if they refuse.

goals.description: Open my course materials and begin reviewing the specific section of the syllabus regarding plagiarism policies and group project accountability clauses to prepare for tomorrow's defense discussion.
goals.rationale: While waiting for the file to actually arrive (which shouldn't take long given my ultimatum), I need to secure my knowledge base. Knowing the rules allows me to negotiate better tomorrow if they try to blame me. Health is secondary right now; I have 6 hours of sleep debt, but grinding while mentally prepared for the confrontation is more efficient than sleeping then panicking.

goals.description: Complete the remaining 14% of my own project share using the newly acquired resources, ensuring zero errors so I can submit a flawless version regardless of what happens with the team's part.
goals.rationale: This is the safety net. Only if the file doesn't arrive by 18:45 will I start this. If the file arrives, I'll integrate it and finish early. If not, I finish my part perfectly anyway. This minimizes risk without sacrificing my current momentum.

goals.description: Immediately draft and send a firm rejection to teammate_A declining their offer, explicitly stating that I will not be taking on their work due to capacity constraints.
goals.rationale: Per my Assertive/Impulsive traits, wasting mental energy negotiating or feeling responsible for their failure is unacceptable. I must set this boundary now to reclaim my focus and protect my academic reputation.

goals.description: Execute the 'Coursework block' routine: complete my specific share of the project (30% remaining) by 19:00 using deep work techniques to ensure high-quality output.
goals.rationale: Academic outcomes are my top priority (Value=0.5). My short planning horizon means I need to secure this deadline immediately rather than worrying about long-term consequences. Completing my share ensures I look competent to the professor.

desires: Finish my own share of the project by 19:00 without letting this extra task derail my progress or make me look incompetent to the professor.
Reject teammate_A's request immediately and assertively so I don't have to waste mental energy negotiating or feel responsible for their failure.
